name: websocket-stability-profiler
description: Comprehensive WebSocket connection stability profiler and analyzer

scripts.profile-quick: node -e "const WSProfiler = require('./src/index.js'); const profiler = new WSProfiler({ testDuration: 60000, maxConnections: 5, thirtySecondTimeoutRate: 0.7 }); profiler.run().then(() => console.log('Done')).catch(console.error);"
scripts.profile-extended: node -e "const WSProfiler = require('./src/index.js'); const profiler = new WSProfiler({ testDuration: 300000, maxConnections: 15, enableMemoryLeak: true }); profiler.run().then(() => console.log('Done')).catch(console.error);"
scripts.mock-server: node -e "const MockServer = require('./src/MockWebSocketServer'); const server = new MockServer({ port: 8080, enableThirtySecondTimeout: true }); server.start().then(() => console.log('Mock server running on port 8080'));"
scripts.dashboard: node -e "const WSProfiler = require('./src/index.js'); const profiler = new WSProfiler({ enableWebInterface: true, testDuration: 180000 }); profiler.run();"
scripts.analyze-existing: node -e "console.log('Use this to analyze existing WebSocket connections in your application');"

author: WebSocket Stability Team
